Hello everybody and welcome to the fourth installment of seven figure emails. This one is called the no problem problem. If it's the first one you're diving into, this is how all of these sessions go down. We're first going to give a little bit of a background on the template. Then we're going to really have a deep dive on the persuasion principles, why it works, why we're writing it the way we're writing it. And then we're going to break down the template itself, including some questions to ask or write about that will really give you the content needed to write the email. And then in the next video below this one, we will take a look at an example so you can see what it looks like within an actual launch. Cool. So let's do it. So the background, this used to be a GIF, but I can't get the GIF to work. So that's kind of a shame. You're only going to have to know what I'm talking about based off this picture. But I think this was from the movie EZA. What's your problem? It was a rhetorical question. Anyway, no more movie trivia. Really this template or this concept of the non-problem problem came from analyzing or just listening to hundreds of sales and enrollment calls over the past few years. I may have mentioned it somewhere else that part of my copywriting process when I'm writing for launches or writing for anyone who's really a coach or an enrollment specialist or even in the SAS world, those who do demo calls is really listening to those calls, especially the successful ones, the ones that actually closed and just kind of analyzing what were those triggers that got people to say, yes, where was there a shift in tone? Where did people get a little more excited? Where did buy-in actually happen? And a lot of the times I noticed that buy-in was happening when the prospect was sold on the future problems that would arise as a result of solving their initial problems. So now we got the gift work. Okay, anyway, good thing I don't need to act it now. So what this is really all about is giving your prospect the gift of better problems. And you're going to see why this works so well in just a second. But first, a marketing and life truth bomb. Every problem your product solves or outcome it enables in turn creates a new set of problems. And this template is all about selling your prospect on the problems they wish they had and that they can and will have as a result of using your product. So there's actually three milestones on a timeline to look at here. First there's the current problems they're experiencing in their life. This is the moment of highest tension which we broke down in the last video. Then there's point B, which is the moment of highest pleasure that they will achieve as a result of using your product. And from that place, after they've solved the initial problems, after they've enjoyed the benefits in a very specific way in point B, new upgraded problems will emerge from that place. So what's that play here and how do we make it super practical in our email copy? Well, the first one is presupposition. So by assuming that they have these new problems in point C, if we could get buy-in on point C, then there's a presupposition that they've already made the trek from point A to point B with your product. It's just assumed. If they're experiencing something in C, it means that by default they solved A and already went to B. Now, here is where things are really exciting. When it comes to writing copy, point A is of course the easiest to get agreement and buy-in on. It's their current reality. If you're reflecting it back with any degree of accuracy, they're going to be nodding their heads and saying, yep, that's exactly what's going on in my life. You nailed it. But here's where things get really interesting. Point C is actually much easier to get buy-in than point B. So it's easier to sell your prospect on new upgraded problems than it is to sell them on the moment of highest pleasure, the benefits that come from using your product. And here's why. Believability and trust consumers are always going to be skeptical by nature. They're always going to be pessimistic and have that kind of negative tint on what your product can do. And therefore are more likely, we're just wired to believe in problems more than positive claims. So by pointing out the legit hyper specific problems that emerge as a result of the new outcomes, the moment of highest pleasure, you increase believability and trust and bypassed the need to oversell the positive and less believable outcomes that preceded. So this is really a Trojan horse. We're essentially encasing the benefits and the moment of highest pleasure within the new problems that will arise as a result of experiencing them. Persuasion principle number three, this is a twist on the good old future pace. Instead of only future pacing, the desirable outcomes in point B, what they're going to experience, we actually future pace a better set of problems, which is point C. Of course, this is the believability factor. We're more likely to believe that we're going to have problems than we are believing that we're going to have, you know, the fairytale ending. So start treating upgraded but still legit problems that will arise with the same amount of weight as the positive outcomes that precede them. So a few message mining questions to extract the content here for the upgraded problems, very, very simple. What are the new upgraded problems that will arise if my product or service delivers its core promise? What will be the new problems during the process of solving their problem immediately after? So once they get that result, what is the new set of problem they have to encounter three months after or even further out in the future, just having a list of those new upgraded problems that arise from getting their initial problem solved through your product is what's going to give you the content for this email. And then for exclusive empowerment, we've already looked at this in previous sessions, but here are those questions again. What has my ideal client already and recently achieved? What actions are my ideal client currently taking or willing to take that should set them up for a better outcome? And what higher aspirations or expectations is my ideal client have for themselves? So having all these questions answered will definitely help you in writing this template effectively. So before we look at the template, where to send it, you're going to want to send this in the final 48 hours of a launch or Evergreen funnel. And it also works really well as a standalone sales email. So a pretty flexible template that you could send, you know, pretty much any time you're trying to sell your product. Here's the actual theme plate. You're going to start by just listing, mentioning it off right off the bat, what the core promise of your product is. You know, use my product and achieve X result. Then you're going to talk about the moment of highest pleasure. This is how you're going to experience that. Um, and then you're going to tell them what they're going to be overcoming the moment of highest tension, which is point A, your product as a difference maker or the bridge between those two points and then an exclusively empowered close. So taking all these elements here, my slides, my slides. What happened? Okay. There we go. Um, taking all these, um, exclusive empowerment notes here using that as a reason that they could take you up on this offer. So that's the exclusively empowered close and then sell the next set of upgraded problems, usually in the PS. This is point C. Um, this is where you can say, this is what you are going to be prepared to encounter once you have this result. This is what's going to happen. And if you could get buy in on point C, it presupposes that they use your product to get from point A to point B and then to point C. So that's it. And then close with the final call to action. So that is the non problem, problem selling your prospect on the future problems. They will encounter because they're more believable than the benefits and moment of highest pleasure that they will enjoy as a result of using your product. So that's what we have in the next video. We break it down so you could see what this looks like in action. So meet us there.
